mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving equity earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and residential housing starts signals broad-based economic acceleration. The joint expansion of supply and demand compresses risk premiums and lifts equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 15 basis points per month over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, rising short-term rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during quarters where FEDFUNDS increases by more than 50 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real consumer credit balances indicate expanding household purchasing power and consumption demand. This fundamental improvement in cash flow expectations drives immediate positive momentum in equity markets over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS changes against 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 across all rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below 110 signals supply chain bottlenecks or capacity constraints. These supply shocks trigger inflationary pressures and earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO drops below 110, or the signal produces false breakdowns in more than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in unemployment and growth in real consumer credit reflect a self-reinforcing labor-consumption feedback loop. This dual expansion sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal's predictive power decays to zero when tested against SPX 60-day returns in a walk-forward validation spanning multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: In high-inflation environments, rising consumer prices erode real disposable income and suppress discretionary spending. This demand destruction forces earnings revisions downward and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat despite CPIAUCSL accelerating above 3.0 percent year-over-year, indicating the mechanism is overridden by liquidity injections.
